chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَلَاةِ الْإِحْرَامِ وَ عَقْدِهِ وَ الِاشْتِرَاطِ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – The Prayer of the Ihraam, and tying it, and the stipulations regarding it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/256)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/256)
text: لَا يَضُرُّكَ بِلَيْلٍ أَحْرَمْتَ أَمْ نَهَارٍ إِلَّا أَنَّ أَفْضَلَ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ الشَّمْسِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, form Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby, and Muawiya Bin Ammar, altogether,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘It does not harm you whether you wear Ihraam at night or by the day, except that the superior of that is at the (start of the) decline of the sun’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Am mar all have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not harmful to assume the state of Ihram during the night or during the day; however, it is more virtuous to do so at Zawal (declining of the sun toward the west at noontime).’”
